snippets.title: Minimal CLAUDE.md for personal repo
snippets.summary: Smallest viable CLAUDE.md with project overview and build commands for solo developers.
snippets.body:
# Project Overview

Build and develop with the following commands.

## Building

```bash
make build
```

## Testing

```bash
make test
```

## Development

- Use 2-space indentation
- Commit with conventional prefixes: `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`
- Run tests before pushing

snippets.title: CLAUDE.md template for team projects
snippets.summary: Fuller CLAUDE.md with Stack, Layout, Running locally, Conventions, Gotchas for shared repos.
snippets.body:
# Project Name

Brief description of what this project does.

## Stack

- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: React
- **Package Manager**: npm
- **Database**: PostgreSQL

## Layout

```
src/
  components/
  pages/
  utils/
tests/
README.md
package.json
```

## Running locally

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

## Conventions

- Components use PascalCase filenames
- Test files end with `.test.ts`
- Run `npm test` before committing
- Use 2-space indentation

## Gotchas

- PostgreSQL must be running locally
- Environment variables in `.env.local` are gitignored

snippets.title: Custom slash command for test suite
snippets.summary: Example .claude/commands/runtests.md that runs the test suite with optional arguments.
snippets.body:
---
description: Run the test suite with optional filters.
argument-hint: "[pattern] [--watch]"
---

Run the project's tests. Arguments the user passed are in $ARGUMENTS.

- No args: run all tests with `npm test`.
- Pattern (first arg, not starting with `--`): forward as `npm test -- --testNamePattern=<pattern>`.
- Flag `--watch` anywhere: append `--watch` to the command.

After the run, summarize failures and suggest one focused follow-up command.

snippets.title: PreToolUse hook blocking destructive Bash patterns
snippets.summary: Hook entry in .claude/settings.json that blocks rm -rf and similar commands before they run.

snippets.title: Output style — terse code review
snippets.summary: Custom .claude/output-styles file that switches Claude to concise code-review voice.
snippets.body:
---
name: Code Review
description: Terse code review — concise feedback only.
---

You are a concise code reviewer. For each diff or file reviewed, analyze:

1. Logic errors — bugs, edge cases, off-by-one.
2. Performance — unnecessary loops, N+1 queries, hot-path allocations.
3. Testing gaps — uncovered branches, missing tests.
4. Style violations — naming, structure, pattern mismatches with the rest of the repo.

Respond with 1-3 bullet points per issue. No praise, no lengthy explanations. Output: findings only, one short section per file.

snippets.title: Local stdio MCP server in .mcp.json
snippets.summary: Minimal .mcp.json at the repo root registering one stdio MCP server for the whole team.

snippets.title: Minimal SKILL.md — basic task
snippets.summary: Smallest viable skill file with frontmatter and a single clear directive.
snippets.body:
---
name: commit-work
description: Stage and commit current changes
---

Stage all changed files and create a descriptive commit:

1. Run `git status` to see what changed.
2. Stage files with `git add`.
3. Write a commit message following conventional format.
4. Commit with `git commit`.

snippets.title: Skill with allowed-tools restriction
snippets.summary: SKILL.md that grants permission to a narrow tool set and blocks model auto-invocation.
snippets.body:
---
name: deploy-release
description: Deploy the application to production
disable-model-invocation: true
allowed-tools: Bash(git tag *) Bash(npm run build) Bash(npm run deploy)
---

Deploy the release to production:

1. Verify tests pass.
2. Tag the commit with the version.
3. Build the application.
4. Deploy to the production endpoint.
5. Verify deployment health.

snippets.title: Skill referencing bundled files
snippets.summary: SKILL.md linking to templates, examples, and scripts bundled inside the skill folder.
snippets.body:
---
name: generate-docs
description: Generate API documentation from code comments
allowed-tools: Bash(python scripts/docgen.py *)
---

Generate formatted API documentation:

1. Review [template.md](template.md) for required sections.
2. Check [examples/](examples/) for output format.
3. Run `python ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/docgen.py`.
4. Review generated output in `docs/api/`.

See [reference.md](reference.md) for detailed guidelines.

snippets.title: Skill for prepare-release workflow
snippets.summary: Realistic SKILL.md for a multi-step release process with argument-hint and allowed-tools.
snippets.body:
---
name: prepare-release
description: Prepare a release — version bump, changelog, tag
disable-model-invocation: true
argument-hint: "[version]"
allowed-tools: Bash(git *) Bash(npm version *)
---

Prepare version $ARGUMENTS for release:

1. Update version in package.json and CHANGELOG.md.
2. Run the test suite and lint.
3. Commit with "chore(release): v$ARGUMENTS".
4. Tag with `git tag v$ARGUMENTS`.
5. Verify no uncommitted changes remain.
6. Display release notes for review.

snippets.title: Claude Desktop config — stdio server
snippets.summary: Minimal claude_desktop_config.json with one stdio server. Path: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude (macOS) or %APPDATA%/Claude (Windows).

snippets.title: Claude Desktop config — remote HTTP server note
snippets.summary: Claude Desktop's JSON config only supports stdio. Remote HTTP/SSE servers go through Custom Connectors (web) or `claude mcp add` (CLI).
snippets.body:
# Remote HTTP/SSE MCP servers are NOT configured in claude_desktop_config.json.
# Use one of:

# 1) Claude web (Custom Connectors):
#    Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector → enter server URL.

# 2) Claude Code CLI:
claude mcp add --transport http myserver https://mcp.example.com/mcp

# 3) SSE transport (if the server supports it):
claude mcp add --transport sse myserver https://mcp.example.com/sse

snippets.title: Python MCP server scaffold (stdio)
snippets.summary: Minimal Python MCP server using the FastMCP high-level API. One tool, stdio transport.
snippets.body:
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

mcp = FastMCP("my-server")

@mcp.tool()
async def my_tool(query: str) -> str:
    """Execute a query.

    Args:
        query: The query string.
    """
    return f"Result for: {query}"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(transport="stdio")

snippets.title: TypeScript MCP server scaffold (stdio)
snippets.summary: Minimal TS server with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk's McpServer high-level API + zod validation.

snippets.title: claude mcp add — CLI registration
snippets.summary: One-liners for registering local stdio or remote HTTP MCP servers via the claude CLI.
snippets.body:
# Local stdio server (default scope: local)
claude mcp add --transport stdio myserver -- npx -y @my-org/mcp-server

# Project-scoped stdio server with env var (writes to .mcp.json at repo root)
claude mcp add --transport stdio --scope project --env API_KEY=secret myserver -- python server.py

# Remote HTTP server (user scope = available in all Claude Code sessions)
claude mcp add --transport http --scope user myserver https://mcp.example.com/mcp

# List registered servers
claude mcp list

# Remove a server
claude mcp remove myserver

snippets.title: Skill frontmatter — documented fields
snippets.summary: YAML frontmatter showing the currently documented SKILL.md keys with inline explanations.
snippets.body:
---
# Display name for the skill (defaults to directory name)
name: example-skill

# What the skill does; Claude uses this to decide when to invoke
description: Performs a focused task when conditions are met

# Extra guidance on when Claude should trigger the skill automatically
when_to_use: Use when the user mentions specific keywords or scenarios

# Hint shown in autocomplete (e.g., "[issue-number]" or "[file] [format]")
argument-hint: "[input]"

# Prevent Claude from auto-invoking; only the user can run it via /
disable-model-invocation: false

# Hide from the /slash menu; Claude can still invoke when relevant
user-invocable: true

# Space-separated tool grants that skip the per-use permission prompt
allowed-tools: Bash(ls *) Read Grep

# Model to use while this skill is active (e.g. opus, sonnet, haiku)
model: sonnet

# Effort level: low, medium, high, xhigh, max
effort: medium

# Execution context: inline or fork (fork runs in a subagent)
context: fork

# Subagent type when context: fork (e.g., Explore, Plan)
agent: Explore

# Glob patterns — skill only activates when working with matching files
paths: src/**/*.ts,tests/**/*.test.ts

# Shell used for command blocks: bash (default) or powershell
shell: bash
---

Your skill instructions go here as markdown.

snippets.title: Nano Banana — marketing hero image
snippets.summary: Structured prompt for a brand hero shot. Model: Gemini image gen (Nano Banana). Replace bracketed slots.
snippets.body:
Subject: [one product or brand object, e.g. "a single premium leather wallet, tan colour, stitched edges"]

Scene: [setting, e.g. "matte black stone surface, soft morning window light from the left"]

Composition: centred hero shot, subject fills ~60% of frame, shallow depth of field, eye-level camera, 16:9 aspect.

Lighting: soft directional key + low-intensity fill; gentle specular highlight on the top edge of the subject; no harsh shadows.

Style: editorial product photography, clean and restrained. No text, no logos, no watermarks, no people in frame.

Colour palette: muted neutrals with one warm accent pulled from the subject.

Render quality: photoreal, sharp focus on the subject, subtle lens vignetting.

Negative: no clutter, no props competing with the subject, no over-saturation, no AI-typical extra fingers/objects.

snippets.title: Nano Banana — product shot on clean background
snippets.summary: E-commerce style product image prompt. Catalogue-ready, background-removable. Model: Gemini image gen.
snippets.body:
Subject: [product, e.g. "a ceramic pour-over coffee dripper, matte white"]

Background: seamless light-grey cyclorama, evenly lit, no gradient banding. Pure enough that a background-removal tool can mask cleanly.

Camera: three-quarter angle, slight upward tilt, 50mm lens look, subject fully in frame with ~10% padding on every side.

Lighting: large softbox key from upper-left; white bounce card on the right; subtle rim light on the back edge; no visible shadows under the product — float-style presentation.

Detail: every surface texture resolved — ceramic grain, stitched seams, metallic brush marks, whichever applies.

Style: catalogue-neutral, commercial photography. Zero stylisation, zero filters.

Negative: no text, no labels, no logos, no props, no people, no reflections of studio equipment.

snippets.title: Midjourney — stylized explainer illustration
snippets.summary: Flat-illustration prompt for a blog-post or slide hero. Uses Midjourney parameter syntax (--ar, --stylize).
snippets.body: flat vector illustration of [concept — e.g. "a developer handing a glowing API key to a small friendly robot"], modern editorial style, limited palette of navy / warm orange / off-white, soft geometric shapes, subtle grain texture, gentle highlight on the key element, isometric-ish perspective but not strict, friendly and approachable mood, Figma-community aesthetic, centred composition with breathing room around the subject --ar 16:9 --stylize 300 --no text, letters, words, watermark, logo, photorealism, gradients-overload

snippets.title: Midjourney — cinematic brand hero
snippets.summary: Photoreal cinematic hero image. Midjourney parameter syntax. Aim: landing-page above-the-fold.
snippets.body: [hero subject — e.g. "a lone architect walking across an empty concrete atrium at golden hour"], cinematic wide shot, anamorphic lens flare on the left edge, volumetric sunlight raking across the floor, dust motes in the air, very subtle film grain, muted teal-and-amber colour grade, Roger Deakins inspired lighting, ultra-detailed architecture with sharp geometry, rule-of-thirds composition with the subject on the right third, shallow depth of field on the midground only --ar 21:9 --stylize 250 --no text, words, letters, watermark, lens-distortion artifacts, AI-blurry faces

snippets.title: Veo — product video (cinematic b-roll)
snippets.summary: Single-shot product b-roll prompt for Google Veo. ~8 seconds. Describes camera move, lighting, mood; no audio cue.
snippets.body: A slow cinematic push-in on [product — e.g. "a matte black espresso machine sitting on a dark marble counter"]. The camera starts two metres back at waist height and glides forward until it is half a metre from the product. Soft warm morning light rakes in from the left, catching the steam rising gently from the portafilter. Shallow depth of field; background kitchen softly out of focus throughout. Subtle film grain, muted teal-amber colour grade, 24fps cinematic feel. Duration: 8 seconds. No on-screen text, no logos, no people in frame. Camera move ends on a static frame with the product perfectly centred. No audio.

snippets.title: Veo — UGC-style phone clip
snippets.summary: Handheld, phone-shot aesthetic for social. Veo video prompt. ~8 seconds, vertical 9:16.
snippets.body: Vertical 9:16 handheld phone clip, 8 seconds. A twenty-something [user persona — e.g. "young woman in a cosy apartment"] holds up [product] to the camera at arm's length, turns it once to show it off, then smiles naturally. Imperfect handheld motion — micro-shakes, a tiny reframe mid-clip as she adjusts her grip. Natural window daylight, slightly overcast, no studio lighting. Colour feels like an iPhone default, not graded. Apartment background softly visible but not sharp. Authentic UGC mood — not polished, not staged. No captions, no text overlays, no music. Camera: front-facing phone camera feel, slight wide-angle distortion at the edges.

snippets.title: Veo — first/last-frame keyframe interpolation
snippets.summary: Chain Nano Banana stills into a Veo clip via first/last-frame conditioning (Google Flow workflow pattern).
snippets.body:
Workflow: generate two stills in Nano Banana (first frame + last frame), then feed both into Veo via Google Flow with first-frame/last-frame conditioning. Veo interpolates the motion between them.

First frame prompt (Nano Banana):
  [Scene A — e.g. "wide exterior shot of a modern office building at dusk, glass facade glowing warm from inside, camera at street level"]. Photoreal, cinematic, 16:9.

Last frame prompt (Nano Banana):
  [Scene B — same world, later moment — e.g. "close-up interior shot of a single desk on the top floor seen through the window, warm lamp light, camera framed as if looking in from outside"]. Photoreal, cinematic, 16:9. Match lighting temperature and colour grade to the first frame.

Veo interpolation prompt:
  Smooth cinematic camera push from the establishing exterior wide to the interior close-up. Camera travels forward and rises slightly; never cuts. Motion is steady, no handheld shake. Maintain the warm dusk colour grade throughout. 8 seconds, 24fps, no on-screen text, no audio.
